title: Albanese Announces New Security Powers To Secure Fuel Supply In Australia | 10 News
description: 'Shiploads' of fuel are on their way, that was the assurance from the Prime Minister today as he announced new measures to keep the bowsers flowing.   Among them offering surety to importers - but there won't be any rules to crackdown on those hoarding fuel Anthony Albanese simply saying that isn't the "Australian Way". title: WA Residents Assess Damage Left In Wake Of Tropical Cyclone Narelle | 10 News
description: Residents in Western Australia's north are beginning to assess the damage left in the wake of Tropical Cyclone Narelle.   Grace Barlow has been following the system’s developments and reports live from Carnarvon. title: Yemen’s Houthi Rebels Reportedly Launch Missiles Towards Israel | 10 News
description: The world's fuel supply heavily relies on the restoration of safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, something Donald Trump implied was a top priority as he called it the Strait of Trump.   But the US may soon have "another" headache with Yemen's Houthi Rebels reportedly launching a strike of their own toward Israel. title: Several People Rescued After Dangerous Swell Smashed NSW East Coast | 10 News
description: The sea has been angry this day but waters along the New South Wales coast have calmed tonight after wild swells put on a spectacular show.  It wasn't quite the "one-in-one hundred year" conditions experts predicted, however several people still needed to be rescued. title: US-Israeli Strike Hits University In Tehran | 10 News
description: According to Iranian Media, a US-Israeli strike hit the Iran University of Science and Technology in Tehran on March 28. Iran’s Revolutionary Guard responded to the strikes, threatening to target US universities in the Middle East. "If the US government wants its universities in the region to be free from retaliation... it must condemn the bombing of the universities in an official statement by 12 noon on Monday, March 30, Tehran time," they said in a statement released by Iranian media. title: Mourners Attend Funeral Of Stranger As Petrol Crisis Prevents His Family From Attending | 10 News+
description: Dozens of people were inspired to fill a funeral chapel on Friday to attend a service for a total stranger.  Most of Michael Purcell’s family were unable to travel from Ireland to bid him goodbye because of the fuel shortage caused by the war in Iran. title: Victoria And Tasmania Introduce Free Public Transport Amid Fuel Crisis | 10 News
description: State and Territory leaders have held emergency talks this afternoon ahead of a National Cabinet meeting tomorrow, addressing the growing fuel crisis. While no nationally mandated measures have been announced, two states are forging ahead with free public transport, hoping that offering millions of Australians a free ride will help ease demand for petrol. title: Australia To Play Key Role In NASA’s Artemis II Moon Mission | 10 News
description: The countdown is on as four astronauts prepare to orbit the Moon later this week for the first time in more than 50 years, and Australia will be playing a very direct part in ensuring the Artemis II mission is a success. NASA plans to use our deep space satellites to keep communication lines open between the space explorers and crews back on Earth. title: Israeli Strike Kills Three Journalists In Lebanon | 10 News
description: The Middle East is marking a grim milestone, one month of the Iran war. There are new fears that the conflict is widening, with Yemen’s Houthi rebels joining the fight, claiming responsibility for attacks on Israel.  Three journalists have been killed by an Israeli strike in Lebanon. title: John Travolta’s Luxury Plane Donated To Aussie Museum | 10 News+
description: In 2017, ‘Grease’ star John Travolta donated a rare Boeing 707 to an Australian aviation museum. But it has proven to be a year-long process of disassembling and sending it across the sea. It’s no ordinary plane either, fitted with custom-made carpet, a double bed, a shower, and a seating area. The hope is that those who come to see the plane will get a taste of luxury. title: Dairy Farmers Push For Return To Glass Milk Bottles Amid Fuel Crisis | 10 News+
description: Dairy farmers, like Shane Hickey, are calling for a return to glass bottles as the fuel crisis continues to impact multiple industries. Plastic milk bottles and their lids are made from oil derived resin, and supply shortages caused by the war in Iran are now contributing to surging milk prices.
